Multi-touch gesture recognizers: pinch (scale) and rotate.

SDL2 fires a MultiGesture event when the OS detects a two-finger pinch or rotation on a touchpad / touchscreen. We expose the deltas via PinchDetector and RotateDetector widgets that wrap a child.

On desktop without touch hardware, these widgets are inert (no pinch / rotate happens). For testing, the runner could synthesize multi-touch events from Cmd+drag, but that's not wired today.

Imports

../foundation/widget, ../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/key, ../foundation/runtime, ../foundation/binding

Types

PinchDetector = ref object of StatefulWidget
  onPinch*: proc (scale: float32) {.closure.}
  onPinchEnd*: proc () {.closure.}
  child*: Widget
Calls onPinch with the cumulative scale factor (1.0 = no change, 2.0 = pinched-out-to-double, 0.5 = pinched in). 
PinchEvent = object
  deltaScale*: float32
  deltaTheta*: float32
  x*, y*: float32
A single multi-touch update from the OS. The runner pushes one of these per SDL MultiGesture event. Detectors that care about scale read deltaScale; rotation detectors read deltaTheta. Cumulative state lives in the detector widget, not the event. 
RotateDetector = ref object of StatefulWidget
  onRotate*: proc (radians: float32) {.closure.}
  onRotateEnd*: proc () {.closure.}
  child*: Widget
Calls onRotate with the cumulative rotation in radians.

Vars

pinchListeners {.threadvar.}: seq[proc (ev: PinchEvent) {.closure.}]

Procs

proc dispatchMultiGesture(ev: PinchEvent) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Called by the runner when SDL fires a MultiGesture event. 
proc pinchDetector(child: Widget; onPinch: proc (scale: float32);
                   onPinchEnd: proc () = nil; key: Key = nil): PinchDetector {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a PinchDetector.

Wraps child so two-finger pinch on touch hardware drives the onPinch callback with the cumulative scale factor (1.0 = no change, 2.0 = pinched outward to double, 0.5 = pinched in to half). onPinchEnd fires when the gesture releases.

On desktop without touch hardware this detector never fires.

proc rotateDetector(child: Widget; onRotate: proc (radians: float32);
                    onRotateEnd: proc () = nil; key: Key = nil): RotateDetector {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a RotateDetector.

Wraps child so two-finger rotation drives the onRotate callback with cumulative radians. Positive radians indicate counter-clockwise rotation, matching the SDL convention. onRotateEnd fires when the gesture releases.

Methods

    forbids: [].}
